question.text: The GOI bond futures contract cycle consists of:

answer.text: 3 serial monthly contracts and 3 quarterly contracts (March, June, September, December cycle)
12 monthly contracts covering the full year

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The trading cycle includes 3 serial monthly contracts (near, mid, far months) plus 3 quarterly contracts from the March/June/September/December cycle. This provides both short-term and medium-term hedging/trading opportunities.
**The Trap:** Only 3 monthly ignores the quarterly component. Only 4 quarterly ignores the monthly component. 12 monthly contracts are not available. The combination gives flexibility for different horizons.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Similar contract cycles exist for equity futures (NISM VIII) where 3 monthly + weekly contracts are available. The quarterly cycle for IRF mirrors global conventions for interest rate products.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The quarterly contracts are particularly useful for hedging at quarter-end dates when many institutional portfolios are rebalanced and reviewed. Monthly contracts serve day-to-day hedging needs.
